Turkish President Erdogan: If there is to be peace in our region, it will be achieved despite this Zionist regime. If stability is to be established in our region, it will again be achieved despite the Israeli government acting with the delusion of the promised lands. If peace is to come to our blood-soaked geography, it will likewise come despite Israel, which ties its own security to the insecurity of others. Because whenever even the slightest hope for peace emerges, Israel will try every way to sabotage it, as it has done many times before. As the front of humanity strives to extinguish the fire in our region, the network of massacre will carry more wood to the flames. Of course, while doing this, they will continue to brazenly target countries that raise the voice of peace, especially Türkiye and Spain. With their hired pens, their media, and the pawns they use as instruments, they will try to silence people of conscience. But no matter what they do, they will not be able to silence brave hearts, nor will they be able to shackle the hearts that defend truth and justice.
Turkish President Erdogan: The Israeli government, which is known to be dissatisfied with the ceasefire, should not be allowed to sabotage the process.

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness ⚡️ 🇮🇷 Iranian opposition media outlets report that the explosion that occurred on Imam Khomeini Street in west Tehran at around 23:40 on Tuesday was caused by an unknown device imploding next to two vehicles, opposite a checkpoint and near a Basij base. Reports are conflicting: opposition media cite locals saying some described the blast as one that was heard several streets away with shattered windows, while others say it was weak. Casualty figures have also varied—from one killed and one wounded, to one killed and five wounded, to no deaths and three wounded taken to the hospital. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 WaPo: The Pentagon is deploying thousands of additional troops to the Middle East as the Trump administration seeks to pressure Iran into a deal while preparing for possible renewed military action if the ceasefire collapses. Around 6,000 personnel are being sent aboard the USS George H.W. Bush and accompanying warships, with a further 4,200 troops from the Boxer Amphibious Ready Group, including the 11th Marine Expeditionary Unit, expected later this month. The influx of forces is expected to align with naval assets already in the region as the two-week ceasefire approaches its expiration on April 22. @wfwitness

Togo Pushes UN to Ditch Distorted World Map Showing Africa Smaller than its Real Size Foreign Minister Robert Dussey urged UN states to replace the Mercator map with Equal Earth projection that shows Africa smaller that it really is, aiming to reflect Africa’s true size & challenge long-standing distortions shaping global perception. NEW: The U.S. is deploying about 10,000 additional troops and multiple warships to the Middle East to increase pressure on Iran. This includes aircraft carriers and Marine units, adding to roughly 50,000 U.S. personnel already in the region. Officials say the military buildup gives the U.S. more options if talks fail, including possible strikes or even ground operations. Source: WaPo

🇮🇷 ⚡️ Jan Fada, For Iran More than 25.4 million Iranians have registered for the “ Jan Fada, For Iran ” volunteer campaign, a state-backed initiative launched in late March 2026 to signal readiness to defend the country against American-Zionist aggression. “Jan Fada” (meaning “self-sacrifice”) allows citizens to sign up online as a symbolic show of support -- it is not conscription , but a commitment expression of national solidarity promoted through media and SMS. Many Senior officials and heads of institutions have also joined, including President Masoud Pezeshkian and Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf , both declaring their readiness to sacrifice their lives for Iran.

British Finance Minister Rachel Reeves: I feel very frustrated and angry that the U.S. went into this war without a clear exit plan. This is a war that we did not start. It was a war that we did not want. Without a clear idea of what they were trying to achieve. As a result, the Strait of Hormuz is now blocked. Source: The Mirror

Spain approved a plan to grant legal status to about 500,000 undocumented migrants, offering them a one-year renewable residence permit. To qualify, applicants must have lived in Spain for at least five months and have no criminal record. Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez called it an “act of justice” and said the move is needed to support Spain’s ageing economy. Source: BBC
